compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM /joe-brainstorm — running decision log (1/N).

GROUNDING (3 research streams, code + prior art):
- Code today is REVIEWER-CENTRIC: a workflow agent step's prompt: is resolved via get_reviewer(id) which RAISES if not in reviewers/catalog.json. 'Reviewer' (distinguished by its 'dimension' field) is the only first-class prompt concept; no resolver path exists for a packaged non-reviewer prompt. No input/output contracts exist on prompts (only front-matter variables/required) or scripted ops (docstrings only). Adding mcp_call as a leaf step is structurally cheap (same shape as scripted: with-in, named-outputs-out).
- PRIOR ART: surveyed BAML/DSPy/Pydantic-AI/Humanloop/LangChain Hub/PromptLayer/promptfoo/MS Prompt flow (prompts) + ComfyUI/Rivet/Flowise/LangFlow/n8n/Windmill/Dagster/Prefect (step palettes) + Dify/Rivet/LangFlow (MCP-as-node). Findings: (a) the proposed category taxonomy (review/verify/transform/code/explore) is shipped by NOBODY — novel, so do not make it the load-bearing primitive; (b) DSPy's signature/module split (typed I/O contract orthogonal to single-turn-vs-agentic execution) is the strongest prior art; (c) visual editors validate wiring by NAMED PORT-TYPE matching + an any/* wildcard, NOT structural JSON-Schema checks at connect time (nobody does that); (d) JSON Schema is the lingua franca for INPUT contracts (Windmill, MCP inputSchema); (e) MCP outputSchema is optional & usually absent — every consumer treats MCP output as opaque/any, so we must default to any.

Q1 DECISION — Prompt model primitive: DECOMPOSED TRIPLE (DSPy-style). A prompt declares input_contract + output_contract + execution_mode (single_turn|agentic) EXPLICITLY; 'category' (review/verifier/transform/code/exploration) is an ADVISORY label used only to group the editor palette. Reviewers are a SUBSET = prompts whose output_contract is the findings schema. Editor validates on the actual contracts, not the category. This resolves item 1's 'reviewers are a subset of prompts' and matches the epic's two separate bullets (specify I/O contract per prompt; distinguish single-turn vs agentic).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (2/N).

Q2 — Contract HOME: FRONT-MATTER LIGHT + SCHEMA-REGISTRY NAMES. A prompt's contract lives in its OWN .md YAML front-matter (input_schema: <name>, output_schema: <name>, execution_mode, category, and reviewer-only dimension/applies_to), but heavy JSON Schemas stay referenced BY NAME in src/rebar/schemas/ (reusing today's output_schema name-indirection). reviewers/catalog.json COLLAPSES into per-file front-matter; the catalog becomes a DERIVED index (scan prompt files to build it), not the source of truth. Editor edits names + text only => item-4 prompt-text write-back stays low-corruption-risk (no giant inline schemas to round-trip).

Q3 — Editor VALIDATION model: TYPE-AWARE EXPRESSION VALIDATION + READ-ONLY INSPECTOR (NOT visualized dataflow ports). Connections stay BPMN control-flow. Extend lint_refs.py: when a step's with: maps a field to ${{ steps.A.outputs.foo }}, check (a) A's output_contract actually produces 'foo' and (b) foo's type is compatible with this step's input_contract for that field; red error at save/lint. Inspector/properties panel shows each step's consumes/produces (read-only) + autocomplete of available upstream outputs when authoring with:. No drawn typed ports (that paradigm is round-trip-fragile — the class of bug branch UX already hit — and a much larger build). Possible future story only if expression-authoring proves too hard.

Q4 — mcp_call step SCOPE: EXTERNAL MCP SERVERS (the novel power), not rebar's own tools. mcp_call targets external servers configured for the workflow (serena/github/Atlassian/etc.) — the capability scripted ops can't provide (Dify/Rivet 'deterministic MCP step' pattern). rebar's own ticket ops stay as scripted ops (no duplication). Brings the hard parts: server-connection config, tool discovery to surface inputSchema in the palette, opaque-output fallback (any) when MCP outputSchema is absent, and an allowlist/sandbox for safety (cf. Flowise RCE CVE-2025-59528). Runtime: rebar runner is Python; pydantic_ai (already the runner) ships MCP-client support to reuse.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (3/N).

Q5 — MCP config/trust: DEDICATED ALLOWLIST + SECRETS, mirroring the prompt two-tier model. rebar SHIPS a default MCP allowlist; projects ADD to it via their own .rebar/ config and store secrets in PROJECT config (never inline). Resolution mirrors prompts: built-in ⊕ project, project-name-wins on collision. A workflow may call only an allow-listed server.tool; else lint/runtime refusal (allowlist IS the trust boundary — Flowise RCE CVE-2025-59528 lesson). Gated by a new REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_MCP_CALL-style env flag, default off. Allowlist entry: name -> {transport stdio|http, command|url, allowed_tools[]}; creds via ${{ secrets.x }}.

Q6 — Prompt write-back location: AUTO-DETECT EDITABLE REBAR CHECKOUT. On Save, detect a writable nava-rebar source tree (pyproject [project].name=='nava-rebar' AND real writable src/rebar/llm/... path). If YES => author/edit the BUILT-IN packaged .md directly (developing rebar). If NO (client project / installed wheel, site-packages read-only) => write project override .rebar/prompts/<id>.md, never touching packaged files; refuse packaged writes when not writable. Matches epic policy with a concrete testable signal. Item-4 requires front-matter-preserving write + round-trip corruption tests.

Q7 — MCP tool discovery for palette: CACHED MANIFEST + EXPLICIT REFRESH. rebar introspects a server once (tools/list), caches tool list + inputSchemas to .rebar/mcp-tools/<server>.json; editor palette reads the CACHE (offline-capable, git-reviewable, deterministic). Explicit 'rebar mcp refresh <server>' (CLI + editor button) re-introspects. Run-time re-validates args vs the live inputSchema to catch drift. Chosen over live-at-edit (online-dependent, no palette when server down) and auto-TTL (unpredictable blocking + cache churn in git).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (4/N) + adopted critique refinements.

Q8a — execution_mode binding: FIXED ON THE PROMPT (not step-level). A prompt intrinsically IS single_turn or agentic; cleaner contract identity + palette can hard-group by it. A prompt usable both ways must be duplicated (accepted tradeoff). single_turn = ZERO tools (pure transform on with: inputs); agentic = current tool-using pydantic_ai path.

Q8b — v1 SCOPE: PROMPT MODEL + CONTRACTS FIRST; MCP + DEEP TYPE-CHECK = v1.1.
  v1 = decomposed prompt model + contract-home + DERIVED-INDEX LOCKFILE (generated, committed) + scripted-op contracts (lowest-risk, built FIRST to anchor the contract vocabulary) + NAME-EXISTENCE expression validation + read-only inspector + prompt library dropdown + editable prompt write-back.
  v1.1 (separable, env-gated) = the mcp_call track (allowlist/secrets/cached-manifest) + DEEP JSON-Schema type-compatibility checking + properties-panel structured fields.
  Rationale: cutting MCP loses no coherence in the authoring story and removes the largest security surface + the type-system balloon-risk from the critical path.

ADOPTED REFINEMENTS from adversarial design review (fold into spec):
1. Validator is THREE-STATE (OK / UNKNOWN-opaque / ERROR) with an 'unchecked' badge; distinct ${{ inputs.* }} namespace for engine-injected vars (ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path) vs steps.*.outputs.
2. Derived index = a GENERATED, COMMITTED lockfile enforcing global invariants (single default reviewer; no dimension collisions; preserve langfuse_prompt/fallback_file/applies_to) — validated at build/validate time, NOT a pure runtime scan.
3. Reviewer membership EXPLICIT (category: review / reviewer: true), NEVER inferred from output-schema shape; schema-match is a validation only (keeps transform prompts out of the security-sensitive close gate).
4. MCP server trust: projects may ADD servers but CANNOT shadow/redefine a built-in server name (invert project-name-wins for the server tier); allowlist pins endpoint IDENTITY not just name; cached manifest advisory + reconciled vs trusted allowlist at run time; no auto-trust of freshly-cloned .rebar/.
5. output_contract changes are BREAKING (override/variant changing output shape needs a new name/version); validate flags override-vs-builtin contract drift; write-back ALWAYS shows the resolved target path before Save.
6. Prompt front-matter is FORMAT-VERSIONED; older binaries detect-and-refuse/strip unknown front-matter (don't render YAML into the prompt body — mixed-fleet safety, like TAG_DELTA). NOTE: shipped reviewer .md files have NO front-matter today (parser exists, files don't use it) => adding it is a migration of every built-in prompt (own workstream).
7. Variant contracts INHERIT unless overridden (override = breaking); validate the RESOLVED contract per variant.
8. 'any'-typed (opaque MCP) output is NOT silently mappable into a typed input_contract — requires an author-acknowledged cast.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (5/N) — OSS build-vs-import & standards review (2nd subagent, Joe-requested).

Bottom line: of 6 build areas, 5 have a maintained lib to IMPORT; only the derived-index/invariants + rebar-specific front-matter extensions are genuinely bespoke. Two things that will SILENTLY bite: mixed JSON Schema drafts, and execution_mode-on-prompt (a conscious divergence).

IMPORT (do not reinvent):
1. MCP CLIENT — reuse pydantic_ai's MCP client (MCPServerStdio/StreamableHTTP, on the official 'mcp' SDK 1.28+) for stdio+http, initialize handshake, tools/list, subprocess teardown. Drop to the 'mcp' SDK ClientSession directly for manifest work (pagination, list_changed, outputSchema validation). Pin pydantic_ai (MCPServer* collapse into MCPToolset, removed in v2). DO NOT write our own transport/handshake.
2. LOSSLESS FRONT-MATTER — python-frontmatter (split body/FM) + a CUSTOM HANDLER delegating to a shared ruamel.yaml YAML(typ='rt'); edit the CommentedMap IN PLACE (never rebuild dicts). PyYAML default re-sorts keys + drops comments = the exact corruption item-4 forbids. Caveat: ruamel normalizes indent/bool/null + loses block-scalar style on REASSIGNMENT (won't-fix) => add a golden round-trip test.
3. JSON SCHEMA SUBTYPE (v1.1 deep type-check) — IMPORT jsonsubschema (IBM, ISSTA'21, Apache-2.0, maintained 2026). isSubschema(producer,consumer). Caveats: targets draft-04 (canonicalize/down-convert our 2020-12 first); returns true/false/UNKNOWN => treat unknown as FAIL-CLOSED (matches our 3-state validator). Hand-rolling subsumption is a multi-month near-undecidable trap.
4. JSON SCHEMA VALIDATION + $ref registry — jsonschema 4.26 + 'referencing' (Registry().with_resources + Draft202012Validator(registry=)). RefResolver is deprecated since 4.18; catch referencing Unresolvable. Avoid fastjsonschema (no 2020-12).
5. CLIENT-SIDE EDITOR VALIDATION — Ajv 8 but import Ajv2020 (not default draft-07-only) + ajv-formats, strict:false (Ajv8 strict THROWS at compile on unknown keywords/formats => breaks live validation). bpmn-js bundles no usable validator; do NOT use @bpmn-io/json-schema-validator (pinned to Ajv6).

CONFORM-TO (standards):
6. JSON SCHEMA DRAFT = 2020-12 EVERYWHERE. pydantic v2 emits 2020-12; MCP SEP-1613 mandates 2020-12. Our hand-written output schemas may be Draft-07 => MIGRATE don't relabel (definitions->$defs, items[]+additionalItems->prefixItems+items; relabeling $schema alone silently mis-validates tuples). Audit docs/output-schemas.md canonical schemas before the registry lands.
7. MCP RESULT SHAPE (2025-06-18): opaque-any is right for no-outputSchema, but ALSO validate structuredContent vs outputSchema when present + feed those fields to the expression validator; handle isError:true; don't read only content[].
8. MCP MANIFEST: follow tools/list nextCursor PAGINATION (else silent truncation) + invalidate on notifications/tools/list_changed; stamp manifest with TTL/version.
9. MCP TOOL ANNOTATIONS (readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint/openWorldHint) drive allowlist UX/defaults — but they're UNTRUSTED hints (default worst-case when absent); the explicit allowlist stays the trust boundary.
10. MCP REMOTE AUTH = OAuth 2.1 for HTTP, env creds for stdio — model the two SEPARATELY; lean on the client lib, don't store bare tokens. Flat 'secrets for auth' under-specifies HTTP.

OWN-IT (bespoke, fine): derived-index lockfile + invariants; step contracts AS JSON Schema (CloudEvents/AsyncAPI/OpenAPI standardize envelopes/transport, not intra-workflow step I/O — JSON Schema by-name is correct, consistent with MCP inputSchema/outputSchema).

BORROW / FIX:
11. FRONT-MATTER VOCAB: align field names with Microsoft Prompty (model/inputs/outputs/description/sample/tags) where they overlap; execution_mode + dimension/applies_to + schema-name indirection are rebar extensions layered on the Prompty-shaped base. Don't invent new spellings.
12. applies_to globs — IMPORT pathspec (GitIgnoreSpec) or 'codeowners'; don't hand-roll; pin which semantics.
13. execution_mode-on-prompt is a REAL divergence from every typed-prompt peer (DSPy/BAML/pydantic-ai all separate typed contract from execution strategy). Joe chose it consciously (one-prompt-one-strategy); document as deliberate, don't claim DSPy parity.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM CORRECTION — RETRACTION of unconfirmed 'decisions'.

User feedback: I (the agent) jumped from research straight to recording dependency adoptions + critique refinements as DECISIONS without discussing tradeoffs/alternatives or getting user confirmation, and without vetting imports against the project bar.

RETRACTED — the following are NOT decisions; reopened as OPTIONS pending explicit build-vs-import discussion:
- ALL the 'IMPORT' verdicts in decision log 5/N (mcp client, ruamel.yaml, python-frontmatter, jsonsubschema, jsonschema+referencing, Ajv2020, pathspec). These were a subagent's verdicts, not user-confirmed, and were not checked against the import bar.
- The 8 'ADOPTED REFINEMENTS' batch-listed in decision log 4/N. These were an adversarial-critique subagent's recommendations; treat each as an OPEN proposal, not an adopted decision.

PROJECT IMPORT BAR (user, explicit): we will NOT import anything that is not BOTH (a) popular on GitHub AND (b) actively maintained. For every capability, BUILD and the relevant STANDARD/PATTERN remain alternatives on the table. The four framings — reinvention, established patterns, standards conformance, peer-handled gotchas — are INPUTS that frame each build-vs-import-vs-conform decision, not an after-the-fact checklist.

STILL STANDING (user-confirmed, NOT retracted): Q1 decomposed-triple primitive; Q2 contract home = front-matter + schema-registry names; Q3 type-aware-expr-validation + inspector (validation DEPTH still open); Q4 mcp_call = external servers; Q5 MCP two-tier allowlist+secrets; Q6 write-back auto-detect editable checkout; Q7 cached manifest+refresh; Q8a execution_mode fixed on prompt; Q8b v1 = prompt model+contracts, MCP+deep-typecheck = v1.1.

NEXT: triage every candidate dependency (+ alternatives) against the import bar with hard metrics; then decide build-vs-import per capability, one at a time, framed by standards/patterns/gotchas. No tickets written yet.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM decision (CONFIRMED by user) — front-matter comments tradeoff.

Machine-managed prompt front-matter will be CANONICAL-serialized (fixed key order, deterministic dump) and will NOT preserve inline YAML comments. APPROVED rationale (user): git history + ticket history supply any meta context needed outside of front-matter and prompt text, so comments inside front-matter are unnecessary. Consequence: we can BUILD a minimal canonical front-matter writer over the already-core PyYAML dep (no ruamel/python-frontmatter) and only ever rewrite the front-matter block, leaving the .md prompt body byte-for-byte. (Open follow-on flagged: must still preserve UNKNOWN/foreign front-matter keys on write-back for forward-compat, and handle line-endings/trailing-newline — round-trip corruptors peers guard against.)

NOTE: this is a genuine user-confirmed decision (unlike the retracted subagent verdicts). Still UNDER DISCUSSION per user pressure-test: (a) whether v1.1 'deep subtype check' is the right approach at all given no standard/lib exists; (b) ajv vs alternatives for in-editor validation; (c) experiments to derisk; (d) overall approach vs OSS editor-round-trip + prompt-library peers.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM — EXPERIMENT RESULTS (derisking spikes, all executed against real fixtures/servers).

E1 FRONT-MATTER WRITER (build-minimal over PyYAML) — ALL PASS. Canonical writer round-trips all 6 REAL reviewer files body-byte-for-byte; idempotent (wb(wb(x))==wb(x)); survives the 3 boundary corruptors (CRLF body, body-starts-with ---, no-trailing-newline + unicode); REJECTS unknown top-level keys; ACCEPTS sanctioned metadata: free-map; rejects BOM; canonical key order. => build-minimal decision derisked; no ruamel/python-frontmatter.

E2 SUBSUMPTION REPLACEMENT — ALL PASS. Proves: validate produced value against the CONSUMER's input_contract catches the missing-required-field wiring bug (validating vs producer's own schema passes = the trap); name-existence alone false-greens; 3-state shallow check flags ERROR on missing required, ABSTAINS UNKNOWN on oneOf, OK when shapes match. Draft202012Validator native. => no subsumption engine; shallow-static + runtime-against-consumer.

E3 MCP TRACK (real serena server via already-present mcp SDK) — ALL PASS. initialize handshake; paginated tools/list (21 tools); ALL advertise inputSchema (JSON Schema for palette); manifest cacheable; call_tool works; BOTH structuredContent and opaque content[] handled; isError surfaces gracefully; bad args don't crash client. FINDING: serena tools report outputSchema present yet a real call returned opaque content[] without structuredContent — so opaque-any fallback is needed EVEN WHEN outputSchema is declared (stronger than the research premise). => v1.1 MCP mechanics derisked; zero hand-rolled transport.

E4 EDITOR VALIDATION LATENCY — PASS. Using rebar's OWN registry-wired Draft202012Validator: build 1.12ms, validate p50 0.46ms / p99 0.52ms. Cross-file $ref (common.schema.json) resolves LOCALLY via referencing.Registry (no network). => debounced server round-trip is imperceptible; NO ajv. FINDING: rebar ALREADY wires referencing.Registry in schemas/__init__.py (registry()/validator()) — the research-recommended pattern — so our contract registry just EXTENDS existing infra (the naive bypass tried to fetch the github $id URL over the network and 404'd; the real code does not).

E5 DERIVED-INDEX LOCKFILE — ALL PASS. Deterministic generation; enforces exactly-one-default + no-dimension-collision (both rejections fire); CI drift gate detects a stale committed index (git diff --exit-code analog). => lockfile pattern derisked.

E8 MERGE MODEL — ALL PASS. Non-overlapping editor-edit + agent-hand-edit on canonical files merge cleanly keeping BOTH edits; overlapping same-line edit yields a normal LOCALIZED git conflict (1 hunk). => authored files merge as ordinary git 3-way text; canonical format IS the mitigation; no special merge driver (only the index is regenerated).

NET: zero new runtime dependencies confirmed across all spikes. Design mechanics validated end-to-end.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM decisions (CONFIRMED by user) — front-matter model FINAL.

1. NO sanctioned metadata / extension area. Front-matter keys are a CLOSED set owned by rebar; the schema evolves by changing rebar itself. (User: sanctioned extensibility is overdesign — our only client is our own editor + workflow engine; I had conflated general-system extensibility with our narrow single-client case.)

2. Unknown keys: WARN + PRESERVE (not reject). Strict rejection's forward-breaking risk (older binary refuses a newer-written file) outweighs strict enforcement. Resolution: an unknown key emits a WARNING and is PRESERVED verbatim on write-back (appended after known keys, deterministic order) so an older writer round-trips a newer file LOSSLESSLY. This is NOT an extensible meta-schema — no validation of unknown content, no user extension API — just 'don't delete bytes you didn't parse.' schema_version remains the cross-version gate; unknown-key handling is the within-fleet graceful-degradation layer. Re-validated in E1: unknown 'future_field' preserved losslessly across rewrite, idempotent over 5 rewrites, canonical order (known first / unknown appended).

3. Front-matter boundary approach = the STANDARD parse-split-rejoin pattern (gray-matter / python-frontmatter), NOT a novel approach (agent's earlier 'novel' framing was wrong — body-verbatim is inherent to split-rejoin). We BUILD the ~40-line split over PyYAML rather than IMPORT python-frontmatter because (a) python-frontmatter is a borderline dep (~419 stars, single-maintainer, wraps PyYAML anyway) and (b) libraries' naive defaults mishandle exactly the 3 boundary corruptors E1 pins by test: body with no trailing newline, body starting with ---, CRLF. No downside to the pattern; the risk was only in blindly trusting a library on edge cases.

STATUS: front-matter model fully settled + spike-validated. Design mechanics complete across all dimensions (prompt model, contract home, validation depth, MCP track, write-back, index, merge). Next: consolidated design doc + child-story breakdown.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM — EPIC REWRITE + MCP SPLIT (user-approved).

Description REWRITTEN from placeholder/provisional charter to the settled FOUNDATION spec (vision fulfilled: editor composes workflows by selecting contract-bearing prompts + scripted ops from a typed palette, and authors prompts in place; every step kind advertises input/output contract + description). Title updated (dropped 'MCP steps', added 'foundation').

MCP track CARVED OUT to follow-on epic: bdac-7cc4-4c10-4425 / pithy-valor-amok ('external MCP-call step kind: allowlist, secrets, manifest'), which depends_on this epic and relates_to e6d9 (workflow-engine-v2). Rationale: separable subsystem, own security surface, env-gated, depends on the contract model but nothing here depends on it.

Both epics pass clarity_check (7/5) + check_ac. Existing story roomy-spine-mirth (editor layout/properties panel) folds into this epic's properties-panel work. NEXT: child-story breakdown under this foundation epic.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PLAN-REVIEW APPLIED (manual run of epic messy-moose-jig's process via its v8 rubric + final_gate_3pass.py harness, against this epic + its 10 live children).

OUTCOME: DET floor PASS (check-ac 10 criteria; clarity 7/5). Three-pass: 31 Pass-1 findings -> Pass-3: 0 BLOCK (v1 advisory posture), 15 advisory, 4 dropped, 12 indeterminate (several 'indeterminate' = aggregate verifier returned no verdict; read on merits). Raw: tmp/foundation_review_3pass.json.

SUBSTANTIVE FINDINGS -> REMEDIATIONS (to apply):
1. [E1/COH/E2/E3/G5 — biggest cluster] Pre-existing story roomy-spine-mirth (4df6) COLLIDES with the new decomposition: duplicates S8's properties panel; elevates branch-legibility to a hard AC while the epic defers branch UX; its bpmn-js-properties-panel wiring reads as contradicting 'zero new runtime deps'; bundles 5 sub-problems. => Re-scope 4df6 to layout/edge-waypoint/nesting ONLY; S8 owns properties panel; branch UX stays deferred (S9); clarify 'zero new deps' = PYTHON runtime deps (editor already vendors bpmn-js).
2. [T8 CRITICAL] execution_mode underspecified: enum, where it lives (prompt front-matter, NOT workflow IR), coexistence with the existing step mode:[findings,structured,text], and the single_turn execution protocol (which API, how with: inputs marshal, output format) are undefined — an implementer must invent them. Epic AC wrongly says 'in the IR'. => Pin in S4 + fix epic AC.
3. [G1G2] resolve_prompt replacement-API signature for get_reviewer left implicit (reviewer vs non-reviewer return); 'Relevant code' paths omit src/rebar/llm/. => Specify signature in S3; fix epic paths.
4. [E1] Catalog REMOVAL + get_reviewer caller-migration unverified (only replacement side tested). => Add S3 AC.
5. [T6/T5b/G6] Editor Save non-happy-paths + write-back robustness: both-locations-unwritable, id collision, atomic write, discard-unsaved, /validate timeout. => Add S6+S7 ACs.
6. [T9] Derived-index lifecycle in editor write-back: authoring must regenerate/invalidate index; delete/retire path; stale-read. => Add S6 AC + S3 retire.
7. [E6 — VISION gap] Success criterion = select prompts AND scripted ops from a typed palette, but no child covers scripted-op palette/insertion. => Add to S6.
8. [E1] Terminology drift (contract vs inputs/outputs front-matter vs input_schema/output_schema decorator). => Add mapping to epic.
9. [G5/E2] S7 validates the JSON textarea that S8 removes — coherence/sequencing. => Clarify S7 target carries to structured fields.
10. [T7/T5e] No doc/ADR deliverable for the new patterns. => Add docs ACs.
11. [T12] schema_version old+new read-side coexistence unstated. => Add S2 AC.
ACCEPTED/NOTED (low pri, internal dev tool): T5d accessibility; T4/T12 rollback = git-revert + config-off.

NEXT: apply remediations across epic + S2/S3/S4/S6/S7 + reconcile 4df6.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PER-CHILD PLAN-REVIEW APPLIED (3-pass v8 harness on each of the 10 children; proportionate scrutiny level=story -> container G3/G4 + task-only criteria dropped; agentic verify on). Raw: tmp/children_review.json.

OUTCOME: 0 BLOCK on every child (advisory posture); ~96 advisory total, but dominated by the SAME cross-cutting themes firing per-child (all overlays forced on, no top-N cap). Triaged into specific defects vs cross-cutting policy.

SPECIFIC DEFECTS (per-child fixes):
1. S3 afe6 [CRITICAL, VERIFIED] resolve_prompt NAME COLLISION: prompts.py:297 already defines resolve_prompt(reviewer,variables,langfuse)->compiled (the render step), and load_prompt + get_reviewer also exist. The new unified resolver must be a DISTINCT name (get_prompt(prompt_id)->Prompt) and S3 must state the fate of all four (get_reviewer removed/folded; existing resolve_prompt render-step retained/adapted).
2. S4 4b2f + S3 [recurring critical] execution_mode DEFAULT/migration: absent execution_mode = agentic (back-compat); S3 migration stamps it on all migrated prompts.
3. S0 5e78 [real] lint must treat an upstream step with NO output contract as UNKNOWN/skip (never error) during incremental rollout; pin the ${{ inputs.* }} engine-var source-of-truth as a named constant.
4. S1 e050 [conf 1.0] What/AC mismatch: What says author INPUT schemas, AC2 requires OUTPUT-schema match + per-op output-shape test -> align (author+register both).
5. S8 a83a [real] specify how a structured field's setValue writes back into rebar:Config; define 'common path'; resolve body('replace') vs AC('common path') coherence.
6. S7 998e [real] define /validate response schema (errors[] path/message); state Save-blocked-on-error / unavailable policy.
7. S6 6592 [real] pin the palette category vocabulary + the bpmn-js insertion mechanism.
8. S2 d25d [recurring critical] schema_version WRITE-side deploy ordering (bump only when emitting a new key; upgrade readers before writers; read-side refuse is the net).

CROSS-CUTTING -> handle ONCE (not 10x churn):
- Docs/ADR (T7 every child): create ONE docs/ADR child story covering all the new conventions.
- Accessibility (T5d every UI child): epic decision = internal dev tool, a11y done-definition explicitly DEFERRED (documented, not silent).
- Rollback/feature-flag (T12/T4 recurring): epic note = internal git-versioned single-deployment tool; rollback = git revert (+ existing config gates for runtime-path changes S4/S5); no per-ticket canary infra.
- Testing failure-paths (E5 recurring): blanket expectation = each child's tests include failure/invalid/empty-path cases.
- G5 slicing: walking skeleton S0 is intentionally cross-layer; S4/S5 multi-layer is cohesive -> keep.

compiled_state.description:
## Why / Vision
The visual editor should let a human **compose workflows by selecting prompts and scripted
operations that carry clear contracts, and by authoring prompts in place**. The unifying
requirement: **every workflow step kind advertises an INPUT contract, an OUTPUT contract, and
a description**, so the editor can present a typed palette and validate edits. Today the
prompt system is reviewer-centric (a workflow agent step's `prompt:` resolves via
`get_reviewer(id)`, which raises unless the id is in `reviewers/catalog.json`), no step kind
declares I/O contracts, and there is no in-UI prompt library or authoring. This epic builds
the contract-bearing prompt/step model and the editor authoring experience on top of it.

This is the FOUNDATION epic. The **MCP-call step track** (external MCP servers as a workflow
step kind) is carved out to a **follow-on epic** that `depends_on` this one — it is a separable
subsystem (allowlist/secrets/manifest), env-gated, with its own security surface, and nothing
here depends on it. The design below was settled via /joe-brainstorm and derisked with spikes
(see the decision-log comments E1–E5/E8: all pass, zero new runtime dependencies).

## What (the settled plan)
- **Decomposed-triple prompt model.** A prompt declares `input` contract + `output` contract +
  `execution_mode` (`single_turn` | `agentic`, fixed on the prompt). `category` is an ADVISORY
  grouping label for the palette. **Reviewers are a subset** identified by an EXPLICIT
  `category: review` / reviewer flag (never inferred from output-schema shape), so transform
  prompts can't accidentally enter the security-sensitive close gate. `single_turn` = zero tools
  (pure transform on `with:` inputs); `agentic` = the current tool-using pydantic_ai path.
- **Contract home = closed-set front-matter + schema-registry names.** Each prompt's `.md`
  YAML front-matter carries the contract using Prompty-aligned spellings (`inputs`, `outputs`,
  `description`, `model`, `tags`) plus rebar extensions (`execution_mode`, `category`, reviewer
  `dimension`/`applies_to`, `schema_version`). Heavy JSON Schemas stay referenced BY NAME in
  `src/rebar/schemas/` (reusing today's `output_schema` name-indirection and the existing
  `referencing.Registry`). Front-matter keys are a CLOSED set owned by rebar; unknown keys
  **warn + preserve** (kept verbatim, appended after known keys, deterministic order) for
  forward-compat — not an extensible meta-schema.
- **Front-matter read/write = parse-split-rejoin built over PyYAML (~40 LOC, no new dep).**
  Split on the `---` fence, parse/mutate the front-matter, re-emit `---\n<canonical dump>---\n`
  + body; **body preserved byte-for-byte**. Canonical key order, LF, single trailing newline,
  no BOM, idempotent. Reviewer-catalog metadata MIGRATES into per-file front-matter (the 6
  shipped reviewers gain front-matter; the parser exists but files don't use it today).
- **Derived-index lockfile.** `reviewers/catalog.json` collapses into a GENERATED, COMMITTED
  index built by scanning prompt front-matter, enforcing global invariants the per-file
  front-matter can't (exactly one default reviewer; no `dimension` collisions; preserve
  `langfuse_prompt`/`fallback_file`/`applies_to`). CI drift gate: regenerate + `git diff
  --exit-code` (the npm-ci / poetry.lock pattern). `applies_to` keeps stdlib `fnmatch`.
- **Scripted-op contracts (built FIRST as the reference impl).** Extend `@register_step` to
  declare `input_schema`/`output_schema` (names) + `description`; backfill the 8 existing ops.
  This anchors the contract vocabulary the prompt model reuses.
- **Validation = name-existence + 3-state shallow static + runtime-against-consumer.** Extend
  `lint_refs.py`: for a `with:` field mapped to `${{ steps.A.outputs.foo }}`, check (a) A's
  output contract produces `foo` and (b) a SHALLOW structural compatibility (top-level required
  presence + primitive kind), returning OK / UNKNOWN / ERROR — abstaining (UNKNOWN) on
  `oneOf`/`anyOf`/`not`/`$ref` (no subsumption engine; that is near-undecidable and has no
  popular maintained lib). A distinct `${{ inputs.* }}` namespace addresses engine-injected vars
  (ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path). At runtime, produced values are validated against the
  **consumer's** input contract via rebar's registry-wired `jsonschema` (the real safety net).
- **Editor inspector + library + authoring.** Connections stay BPMN control-flow (no drawn
  dataflow ports). The inspector shows each step's consumes/produces (read-only) + autocompletes
  available upstream outputs. A **library dropdown** lists built-in + project prompts; the user
  can **select** one, **create a new** prompt, and **edit prompt text**. On Save, write-back
  AUTO-DETECTS a writable `nava-rebar` source checkout (→ edit the packaged built-in `.md`) vs a
  client project (→ write a `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override; never touch read-only
  site-packages); the resolved target path is always shown before Save. An override (or variant)
  that changes a prompt's `output` contract is a BREAKING change (new name/version);
  `validate` flags override-vs-builtin contract drift. Round-trip golden tests prove no corruption.
- **In-editor JSON validation + help.** Live validation of the step config field via a debounced
  server round-trip to the existing Python `jsonschema` (single source of truth, no client/server
  validator drift; measured p99 ~0.5ms so it's imperceptible — no `ajv`/Monaco validator added).
  Per-kind help walks element types + expected JSON shape (driven by the contracts/descriptions).
- **Properties panel (structured per-field entries).** Once contracts exist, replace the single
  config-JSON textarea with structured fields (uses/prompt/mode/model/with/loop bounds). The
  existing story `roomy-spine-mirth` (editor layout/nesting/properties panel) folds in here.
- **Branch/connection UX.** Keep the current representation (condition labels + default-flow slash
  + "after" continuation; no synthetic gateways — round-trip-fragile). Optional, lower-priority
  revisit only if a clearer representation is wanted; underspecified by design.

## Terminology (one model, three surfaces)
A step's CONTRACT = its declared input + output shapes. For PROMPTS the contract lives in front-matter
keys `inputs`/`outputs`; for SCRIPTED OPS it is the `input_schema`/`output_schema` args on
`@register_step`. In BOTH cases the value is a NAME resolving to a JSON Schema in `src/rebar/schemas/`.
"contract", "input/output contract", the front-matter `inputs`/`outputs`, and the decorator
`input_schema`/`output_schema` all denote this same thing at different surfaces.

## Cross-cutting decisions
- **Zero new *Python runtime* dependencies** (validated across all spikes). The editor already VENDORS its
  front-end bundle (bpmn-js + bpmn-js-properties-panel, built to `editor_assets/dist/`); using those
  vendored packages is NOT a new dependency. The constraint is about Python runtime deps. Reuse the already-present
  PyYAML (core), `jsonschema`+`referencing` (registry already wired in `schemas/__init__.py`),
  and stdlib `fnmatch`. Build the small bespoke pieces (front-matter writer, derived-index +
  invariants, shallow compat check). Rejected: ruamel.yaml / python-frontmatter / jsonsubschema /
  pathspec / ajv (fail the import bar or already solved by existing deps/stdlib).
- **Conform to JSON Schema 2020-12 everywhere** (pydantic v2 + MCP both emit it; rebar's schemas
  and the registry are already 2020-12). Migrate any stray Draft-07 (don't relabel).
- **`schema_version` stamped from day one**; older binaries gate on it (like TAG_DELTA, upgrade
  reconcile hosts first). Ship `.gitattributes` pinning prompt/index files to `eol=lf`.
- **`execution_mode` fixed on the prompt** is a conscious divergence from DSPy/BAML/pydantic-ai
  (which keep the typed contract swappable from the execution strategy); documented as deliberate.

## Out of scope (→ follow-on MCP epic)
The `mcp_call` step kind (external MCP servers), the two-tier allowlist + secrets/trust model,
the cached tool manifest, and MCP output handling (opaque-`any` + structuredContent/isError).
That epic `depends_on` this one and is env-gated (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_MCP_CALL`).

## Relevant code
- Prompts/resolver: `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` (`parse_front_matter`, `resolve_prompt`,
  `_glob_match`), `reviewers/catalog.json` + `reviewers/*.md`; runtime resolution in
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py` (RunnerAgentStep — reviewer-centric, generalize).
- IR + validation: `src/rebar/schemas/workflow.v2.schema.json`,
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/{schema,lint,lint_refs}.py`. Scripted ops: `.../workflow/steps.py`
  (+ `@register_step` in `executor.py`).
- Schema registry: `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` (`registry()`/`validator()` — already
  `referencing.Registry` + `Draft202012Validator`; extend with input/contract schemas).
- Editor: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` (+ `resolve_prompts`) + `editor_assets/`; serializer
  `.../workflow/bpmn.py`.

## Context
Settled via /joe-brainstorm with prior-art research, an adversarial design review, a build-vs-import
triage against the project import bar (popular-on-GitHub AND actively maintained), and six derisking
spikes (E1 front-matter writer, E2 validation, E3 real-serena MCP mechanics, E4 validation latency,
E5 derived-index, E8 merge) — all recorded in this epic's decision-log comments. The earlier
"placeholder/provisional" framing is superseded by this spec.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified prompt model: a prompt declares `input`/`output` contracts + `execution_mode`;
      reviewers are an explicitly-flagged subset; built-in non-reviewer prompts are first-class
      and resolvable (project `.rebar/prompts/` override > built-in packaged).
- [ ] Prompts AND scripted ops each declare input contract + output contract + description,
      surfaced in the editor palette/inspector.
- [ ] `single_turn` vs `agentic` represented as a closed enum in the PROMPT FRONT-MATTER (distinct from the
      existing step `mode:[findings,structured,text]`), surfaced in the editor, and honored by the runner.
- [ ] Front-matter writer: canonical, body byte-for-byte, idempotent, unknown-key warn+preserve,
      with round-trip golden tests over the migrated built-in prompts (no corruption).
- [ ] Derived-index lockfile generated + committed, invariants enforced, CI drift gate fails on staleness.
- [ ] Validation: name-existence + 3-state shallow static check at author time; runtime validation
      against the consumer's input contract; engine-injected `${{ inputs.* }}` namespace resolved.
- [ ] Editor: select a prompt from a library dropdown (project + built-in); author a new prompt
      saved to the correct location (auto-detected, path shown); edit prompt text with safe write-back.
- [ ] In-editor JSON validation (debounced server round-trip) + per-kind element/contract help.
- [ ] Properties panel: structured per-field entries driving the step config (no raw JSON textarea).
- [ ] Zero new Python runtime dependencies; schemas conform to JSON Schema 2020-12; `schema_version` stamped.
- [ ] Docs/ADR deliverable: the front-matter format + closed key set, the `execution_mode` divergence (ADR),
      the `.rebar/prompts/` override mechanism, and the CI drift gate are documented (docs/ + CLAUDE.md as fit).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A human can compose a workflow end-to-end in the editor by selecting contract-bearing
      prompts and scripted ops from a typed palette, authoring a new prompt in place, with edits
      validated before Save and the v2 IR round-trip preserved.
- [ ] The reviewer-catalog → front-matter + derived-index migration preserves all existing
      reviewer behavior (resolution, defaults, file routing) with the v2 IR round-trip intact.

## Cross-cutting policy (from the per-child plan-review)
- **Accessibility:** the workflow editor is an INTERNAL developer tool; a full a11y done-definition is
  DEFERRED as an explicit, documented decision (not silent) — revisit if it becomes user-facing.
- **Rollback / feature-flag:** rebar is an internal, git-versioned, single-deployment tool, so rollback
  = git revert. Runtime-behavior changes (S4 runner `execution_mode`, S5 executor runtime-validation)
  sit behind the existing config/gate pattern; no per-ticket canary/flag infra is built.
- **Test failure-paths:** every child's tests include failure/invalid/empty-path cases, not only the happy path.
- **Documentation:** the new conventions are captured in a dedicated docs/ADR child story (below).
compiled_state.title: Workflow authoring v2: contract-bearing prompt/step model + editor authoring (foundation)
